decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the opponent's hand and board while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade. The deck utilizes discard spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress to disrupt the opponent's strategy and removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict to deal with threats. Mishra's Factory provides additional creature threats and Wasteland helps to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay by using efficient creatures like Nantuko Shade and Hypnotic Specter to disrupt the opponent's hand with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy. Mishra's Factory provides additional threats while Dark Ritual allows for explosive starts. Removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict help clear the way for attacks. The deck also has some disruptive elements with cards like Wasteland and Cursed Scroll. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive black creatures and disruption spells, while maintaining card advantage with cards like Graveborn Muse and Cabal Therapy. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Snuff Out and Diabolic Edict, while pressuring the opponent with efficient threats like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade. The combination of early aggression and disruptive capabilities make it a formidable force in the Premodern format, allowing it to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of playing Phyrexian Dreadnought and then using Stifle to counter its ability to sacrifice creatures, allowing a powerful 12/12 creature to stay on the battlefield. The deck also includes various card draw and counter spells to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. It aims to control the game early on and win quickly once the combo is in place, by overwhelming the opponent with a massive threat that is difficult to deal with.

The deck is focused on getting low-cost, fast goblin creatures out on the battlefield quickly to overwhelm the opponent with pure aggression. With cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat creatures into play and cards like Goblin Warchief to speed up your goblin army, the goal is to hit hard and fast before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. Cards like Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Matron help to keep the goblin train chugging along, while removal options like Mogg Fanatic and Gempalm Incinerator can help clear the way for your creatures to get in for damage.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources while utilizing the Oath of Druids card to cheat out powerful creatures onto the battlefield. It includes cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's mana base, along with Sphere of Resistance to slow down their spells. The deck also includes cards like Swords to Plowshares for removal and Sylvan Library for card advantage. The main win condition is to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures like Terravore and Mishra's Factory while controlling the game with Winter's Grasp and Thermokarst.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble and protect the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat while disrupting the opponent's game plan through countermagic and card selection. The deck aims to control the game early on with efficient spells like Counterspell and Daze, while also utilizing card draw and manipulation tools like Opt and Portent to find the combo pieces. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively by leveraging its combo potential and disruption capabilities.

The deck focuses on using counterspells and card manipulation to control the game while assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to cheat out a powerful creature. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own win condition through a combination of countermagic and card draw. The key strategy is to quickly find and play Phyrexian Dreadnought and use Stifle to bypass its drawback, creating a powerful threat that can win the game in just a few turns.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize a combination of cards to quickly put a Phyrexian Dreadnought into play, while also using cards like Stifle and Counterspell to disrupt the opponent's game plan. By efficiently cycling through the deck with cantrips like Opt and Vision Charm, the deck can quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game. Additionally, cards like Thwart and Daze provide additional ways to protect the combo and maintain control of the game.

This deck aims to quickly disrupt the opponent's mana base using cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland, while also establishing a threat on the board with aggressive creatures like Terravore, Nimble Mongoose, and Wild Mongrel. The deck also includes Armageddon to further disrupt the opponent's resources, paired with cards like Sylvan Library and Weathered Wayfarer to maintain card advantage and consistency. The inclusion of utility cards like Swords to Plowshares and Naturalize helps deal with opposing threats, making it a well-rounded aggressive deck focused on resource denial and fast, efficient wins.

The RG Aggro deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay, utilizing efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Quirion Dryad along with burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and Incinerate to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. The deck also includes cards like Call of the Herd and Treetop Village to provide additional threats and pressure. With a mix of creature aggression and direct damage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on using cards like Attunement, Intuition, and Frantic Search to rapidly cycle through the deck and find key combo pieces like Replenish, Opalescence, and Parallax Wave. By efficiently setting up this combo, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful board state, utilizing Parallax Wave to control the battlefield and Opalescence to turn enchantments into threats. Support cards like Swords to Plowshares and Decree of Silence help stall opponents while the combo pieces are assembled. Ultimately, the deck aims to quickly close out the game before opponents can react effectively.

The deck is focused on controlling the game through counterspells and removal spells while utilizing the powerful creature Psychatog to finish off opponents. It features a mix of card draw, disruption, and land destruction to maintain board control and outvalue opponents. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a solid board presence with Psychatog, using its ability to grow larger and potentially one-shot opponents when the time is right. By managing resources carefully and making efficient plays, the deck seeks to outmaneuver and outlast opponents in a battle of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent by using low-cost burn spells and aggressive creatures. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent with a steady stream of damage, while also having the ability to finish off the game with powerful finishers like Ball Lightning and Fireblast. The inclusion of cards like Pyrostatic Pillar and Sulfuric Vortex helps to disrupt the opponent's strategy and ensure that the deck can close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on quickly cycling through its cards to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game. By using cards like Attunement, Intuition, and Frantic Search, the player can search for and fill their graveyard with key combo pieces such as Opalescence, Parallax Wave, and Decree of Silence. Once these pieces are in the graveyard, Replenish can bring them back to the battlefield, enabling the player to overwhelm their opponent with a powerful board presence and lock down the game with Parallax Wave and Decree of Silence.

This deck aims to quickly deploy Phyrexian Dreadnought and counter any attempts to remove it from the battlefield with cards like Counterspell, Foil, and Stifle. By using cheap card draw spells like Gush and Opt, the deck can quickly assemble the combo and protect it from disruption. The key strategy is to efficiently disrupt the opponent's game plan while advancing towards a quick victory with the powerful Phyrexian Dreadnought. With a focus on countering and stifling key plays, this deck aims to control the game and win through its combo synergy.
